Admission Date:  2009-06-12              Discharge Date:   2008-09-22

Date of Birth:  1992-03-30             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:

Attending:Timothy
Chief Complaint:
Fever

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Right internal jugular central venous catheter

History of Present Illness:
60 yo M w PMHx of TBI, sz disorder, heart failure, DVT, BPH with
chronic indewlling foley and frequent UTIs is here transferred
from St. Meriter Hsptl hospital for lethargy fever, confusion and
tmax of 105. Labs at OSH with mildly elevated WBC 12.4,
otherwise no abnormalities. Initially, concern for meningitis
was raised, however no LP performed. He was given 2gm
ceftriaxone IV toradol head ct negative and negative CXR.
Transfered to Dover Behavioral Health System for further work up.

In the ED, initial vs were: 100.8 92 100/p 20 100%. He was
answering questions appropirately, no neck stiffness, full range
of motion, neuro exam was non-focal. Labs notable for wbc 15.8
with 5%bands. Lactate 3.7, K 2.7.  UA. EKG: normal sinus rhythym
. He was given Morphine 4mg, Azithromycin 500mg, Vancomycin 1g,
Potassium 20mEq and tylenol. He became hypotensive to 79/46 was
resuscitated with 4L of fluid, mid to low 80s. Triple lumen IJ
placed in right side. Put out 500cc urine and admitted to ICU.
Vitals at transfer temp 99.2 88/46 HR72 95-96% on 2L. . Total
IN/OUT in ED: 5750/1300

Of note, patient had a recent admission 1990-04-29 for AMS
thought 06-12 UTI and abd wall cellulitis which improved with abx.
At that point, he was started him on methenamine for UTI ppx.

On the floor, vitals were BP126/88 p 70 rr12 sa02 100%3LNC.
Patient complained of sneezing but was without other complaints.
He denied headache, neck stiffness, photophobia, dyspnea, chest
pain, abdominal pain.

Past Medical History:
"Organic brain syndrome" - per sister h/o SDH due to brain
injury in 2011 with resultant cognitive dysfunction
Seizure disorder - per sister last Sz in 1980
Congestive heart failure with EF of 40%
DVT s/p IVC filter on Coumadin.
BPH with Urinary retention with indwelling foley
Recurrent UTIs
Venous insufficicency
Bipolar disease
S/P right hip fracture 02/1981

Social History:
Nonambulating. wheelchair bound. Lives at JenkinsNursing
Home.

Family History:
His mother having died from cancer ~in 1990. His father was a
policeman and died many years ago (etiology unknown). His sister
is his only close relative

Physical Exam:
Vitals: T BP126/88 p 70 rr12 sa02 100%3LNC
General: Alert, pleasant, answering questions appropirately
breathing comfortably no acute distress
HEENT: EOMI PEERLA Sclera anicteric, MMM, oropharynx clear
Neck: supple, JVP not elevated, no LAD
Lungs: L>R ronchi with wheezes on left base. No rales.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs, rubs,
gallops
Abdomen:  Well healing midline surgical scar. soft, non-tender,
non-distended, bowel sounds present, no rebound tenderness or
guarding, no organomegaly
GU: foley in place
Ext: Chronic venous stasis changes BL lower extremities, warm,
well perfused, 2+ pulses, 1+ Edema to mid calf BL.
Neuro: CN II-XII grossly intact, strong handgrip BL, 11-05
strength in upper ext BL, 3/5 strength in lower ext BL.

CXR 2009-06-12: Evaluation limited due to low lung volumes, kyphotic

position and patient's chin obscuring the right lung apex.
Allowing for these limitations, cardiomegaly is again noted with
mild interstitial pulmonary edema. There is a chronic upper
lumbar vertebral body fracture. Right rib cage and left clavicle
shaft deformity again noted.

CXR 2014-03-25: Right internal jugular line tip is at the level of
cavoatrial junction. There is no change in the cardiomediastinal
silhouette including cardiac engorgement. The patient is in mild
interstitial edema. Nodular opacity projecting over of the left
second anterior rib is redemonstrated and as was previously
mentioned, should be further evaluated with anterior shallow
obliques.

Portable Abdominal Xray 1993-10-23: Improved ileus

2009-06-12 5:20 pm URINE     Site: CATHETER

Brief Hospital Course:
60 year old male with history of TBI, seizure disorder, heart
failure, DVT, BPH with chronic indewlling foley and frequent
UTIs who was transferred from St. Meriter Hsptl hospital for
lethargy, fever, and confusion.  He was admitted to the ICU for
hypotension/sepsis.

#. Sepsis and Hypotension due to Pseudomonal UTI:
He was hypotensive on admission felt to be from a pseudomonal
UTI.  He had a right IJ placed in the ED and was given 5L normal
saline.  He was also briefly on Levophed due to hypotension.  He
was treated initially with vancomycin, cefepime, flagyl, and
azithromycin for possibility of pneumonia as well given
increased interstitial markings on chest xray.  His pressors
were stopped soon after admission and his blood pressure
remained stable after antibiotics were initiated.  When his
urine culture results were obtained, his antibiotics were
narrowed to cefepime to cover pseudomonas; this briefly changed
to cipro and then eventually to oral cefpodoxime (cipro was
discontinued due to concern for a lower seizure threshold as
well as potential interference with INR).  He should complete a
7 day course of antibiotics - last day 1980-07-01.
His recurrent UTIs were discussed with his urologist, Dr
Roberts, via email.  His chronic foley was removed.  Instead, he
will wear a condom cath and undergo clean intermittant
catheterization Helen Hayes Hospital.  A log should be kept with his urine
output.  He will follow up with urology.  He should start
macrobid 100 mg daily once he completes his course of
cefpodoxime to try to prevent recurrent UTIs in the futire.

#. Seizure disorder and bipolar disorder:  He was continued on
phenytoin, gabapentin, divalproex, and carbamazepine.
Zonisamide was also restarted as patient stated that he had been
taking it home; this will need to be clarified as an outpatient.

#. Thrombocytopenia: Felt to be related to acute infection.
Should have a follow up CBC to ensure resolution as an
outpatient.

#. Chronic Systolic CHF (EF 40%): His Lasix was started on
admission due to sepsis/hypotension and restarted on he was
stable.

#. H/o DVT: On coumadin s/p IVC filter.  Continues on coumadin.
INR subtherapeutic at discharge (1.5).  He will need an INR
check on 03-08 with adjustment of his coumadin dosing PRN to
achieve INR = 01-10.

#. BPH: His tamsulosin was initially held in the setting of
hypotension, but was restarted 1 day after admission.

#. Code Status: Full code

#. Communication: Patient Sister Patricia Strong: Daniel Johnson Barrett cell
phone # 833-372-7544, brother in law Lee Hatfield Daniel Johnson: 875-448-3555

Medications on Admission:

Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
JenkinsNursing Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Urosepsis
UTI - Pseudomonas


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Confused - sometimes.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Out of Bed with assistance to chair or
wheelchair.

Discharge Instructions:
You were admitted to the intensive care unit with a urinary
tract infection.  This was likely due to having a chronic foley
catheter in place.  We have removed the catheter - instead, you
will wear a "condom catheter" and have clean intermittant
catheterizations twice daily to fully empty your bladder.
You will complete a 7 day course of ciprofloxacin for the
infection.  Then, you will begin taking a medication called
Macrobid 100 mg po daily to prevent future infections.  Your
other medications are unchanged.
